ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF ISSUES

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION, 503, DEFENDANT CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS UNTIMELY. DEFENDANT FAILED TO PROVIDE PROPER NOTICE REQUIRED BY C.C.P. 437C(A), 1010.6(A)(4) AND CRC 2.251(H). DEFENDANT'S PROOF OF SERVICE DEMONSTRATES IT ELECTRONICALLY SERVED ITS MOTION NOVEMBER 13, 2015. WITH A JANUARY 28, 2016 HEARING DATE, THAT IS INSUFFICIENT NOTICE. PLAINTIFFS' OBJECTION BASED ON TIMELINESS IS SUSTAINED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG, MATTER NOT REPORTED

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-28-2016 Denied
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Geoff T. Sloniker of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Erene Bollinger, as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Richard Bollinger, deceased, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT CALAVERAS ASBESTOS LTD.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Calaveras Asbestos, Ltd.'s motion for summary judgment or in the alternative summary adjudication is denied as untimely. Defendant failed to provide proper notice required by C.C.P. 437c(a), 1010.6(a)(4) and CRC 2.251(h). Defendant's proof of service demonstrates it electronically served its motion November 13, 2015. With a January 28, 2016 hearing date, that is insufficient notice. Plaintiffs' objection based on timeliness is sustained. Order signed in open court. Judge: Garrett L. Wong, Matter not reported (503/GLW)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Clark Equipment Companys Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-05-2016 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Clark Equipment Company's motion for summary judgment is off calendar. Defendant was dismissed October 29, 2015. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)
